metadata.name: Tangentum #233
metadata.description:
We live in a multiverse: an infinite-dimensional space of variants. Everything exists here. Everything is possible. Each subset of a multiverse has its own unique laws of physics, a set of rules, and its own distinct features.

Tangentum creates a projection of a random multiverse subset to a 2-dimensional plane. Each variation is a unique representation of the force field, that exists somewhere at some point in time.

This work starts a series of my visual exploration of functions, continuity, and boundedness, and I would love to share it with you.


Please use fullscreen or download artwork in high resolution for a better viewing experience.

Tested with Chrome 107, Safari, and Firefox. Chrome is recommended, other browsers are slower on this artwork.

Saving with high and ultra-high resolution may take a while, please be patient.


Made with love and p5js


Controls in fullscreen:
[1] - save PNG 2700 x 1800
[2] - save PNG 5400 x 3800
[3] - save PNG 10800 x 7600
[4] - save PNG 16200 x 11400

metadata.name: twofrens.exe #240
metadata.description: just two best frens hanging out in cyberspace

metadata.name: Botanical Ghosts #74
metadata.description:
This is a work about growth, impermanence and decay. Ghostly plants and flowers grow around an invisible point, but they immediately begin to fade into the dark background and are soon replaced and covered by others. This process of accumulation and disappearance builds an ever-changing floral garland, or perhaps a funeral wreath.

The monochrome vegetation is part of a fictional flora, born out of random algorithmic combinations, and does not intend to reproduce any real species. It belongs to an “artificial nature” which, like so many worlds that the computers generate for us in movies, games, and virtual reality environments, is a convincing simulation of life, but it is not actually living. AI bots, virtual assistants and non-player characters are also representatives of the uncanny realm of the not-really-alive-but-looking-like-it, which are becoming an increasingly relevant presence in our everyday lives.

These plants and flowers speak about life as a process of incessant renovation, but also about death as the background that’s always there and makes life possible. This spectral vegetation seems to thrive precisely at the verge between both worlds.
_______________


This work is animated and will adapt to any screen size. It generates a never-ending ring of algorithmic foliage, which never repeats itself, and is different for each token. The image preview is triggered after the first five plants or flowers.
Made with p5.js

Controls:
[SPACE] to play/pause
[f] to toggle fullscreen
[s] to download capture

metadata.name: overloaded #15
metadata.description: Overloaded attempts to be a system to explore time perception, through non-representative images, while still representing pixels as upscaled forms. It plays with permanence and non-permanence.

metadata.name: Loom #402
metadata.description:
A unique sketch of an imaginary weave. Color palette inspired by the great works of Anni Albers, Gunta Stölzl, Sophie Taeuber-Arp, or Hilma af Klint.

Experimental genesis token on fxhash made with p5.js and a custom shader.

Original artwork by Andreas Rau · Oslo, 2021

metadata.name: TendysOS #318
metadata.description:
CONTAINS:
- 1(one) Fully-Baked Chicken Tendercoin
- 1(one) of 7(seven) unique SOS varieties:
    ~ Red
    ~ Orange
    ~ Yellow
    ~ ChknDpn
    ~ SpIcY
    ~ God's Img
    ~ Frbdn

*Each Chicken Tendercoin consists of a procedurally generated, geometric mesh as well as a randomly wrapped, photo surface texture.
**This item contains p5.min.js.

Created by Huw Messie, fxhash.xyz, 2021.

metadata.name: Reading a book #532
metadata.description:
The formal structure of a book is beautiful by itself, but its aesthetic is the tacit atmosphere within which events take place in a story.

The WebGL real-time animation sets up an initial grid based on the fxhash. The grid cells get subdivided into another grid and the resulting cells become filled with pixels in color (~33%) or black and white (~66%).
The colored pixels come in 3 different coloring styles.
- Big Palette (~49%)
- Small Palette (~49%)
- Monochrome [b/w] (~2%)
4 noise algorithms modify the pixels of selected grid cells while at the same time 4 reset algorithms reassemble the initial image. The selected cells switch over time.

metadata.name: PotatOSTX #99
metadata.description:
PotatOSTX

CONTAINS:
- 1(one) Fully-Staked, Fully-Potautomated, Homestyle Expression of PotatOSTX
- 1(one) of 12(twelve) unique SOS/SSN flavor combinations:
~ Red
~ Spicy Cinnamon
~ Cherry Pie
~ Blood of Ram
~ Green
~ Blue
~ Indigo
~ Violet
~ Honey Garlic
~ Nacho Cheese
~ Cool Ranch
~ Cinnamon Sugar

*Each PotatOSTX expression consists of a procedurally generated, geometric mesh as well as one randomly wrapped and one procedurally generated image texture.
**This item contains p5.min.js.

Created by Huw Messie, fxhash.xyz, 2022.
